behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: The failure boundary is the handoff between request-building logic and the API client payload shape. A pure unit test of one helper can miss dropped/mis-mapped fields at module wiring boundaries.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the classifier with the real request-construction path and a test double API client that records outbound payloads. Invoke classify() and assert the captured request contains every taxonomy label exactly once in the field the external API actually receives.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily local normalization/validation logic at one object boundary. No real I/O or multi-module wiring is required to trigger the failure.
behaviors.test_description: Call the response-mapping/classification-result handler with an out-of-taxonomy label (for example, "medical") and assert the returned value is exactly "unknown".

behaviors.description: The classification result for the same document is the same across repeated calls.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real model behavior across repeated invocations; nondeterminism is the core risk. Mocked or deterministic fakes cannot reveal true output variance.
behaviors.test_description: Use the real AI classification API with fixed production-like settings and submit the same document many times (for example, 30-100 runs). Assert labels are stable (or within an allowed variance threshold if specified). Record run-to-run variance as failure evidence.

behaviors.description: The number of tokens consumed for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: The real failure is cost/token accounting against the actual external API tokenizer/usage reporting. Local mocks can hide mismatches between estimated and billed tokens.
behaviors.test_description: Send representative documents through the real API path in a controlled test environment and read actual usage/token metadata returned by the provider. Assert total tokens per call do not exceed the configured budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: The key risk is retry-policy wiring across classifier + API adapter + backoff/timer components. This can be caught without real external infrastructure by orchestrating module interactions and timing behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Run classifier with real retry/backoff code, a controllable clock/sleeper, and a scripted API stub that returns rate-limit error first, then success. Assert the call does not fail immediately, waits for backoff/retry-after, retries the expected number of times, and returns the eventual successful label.
